channel.title: Drupal Core
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal Core.

item.title: #3597406: [regression] DrupalApplication (for `dr`) needs to support DRUPAL_DEV_SITE_PATH env var
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +6/−3 · php +6/−3
  • Diff: 09d9f4d
  • Discussion: #3597406 · 3 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

A regression introduced in #3453474 (which added DrupalApplication and deprecated BootableCommandTrait) accidentally dropped support for the DRUPAL_DEV_SITE_PATH environment variable. That variable is the standard way for functional tests to tell the dr command which site directory to use. Without it, recipe-related functional tests in projects such as Drupal CMS could no longer isolate dr to the correct test site. This fix restores the env-var lookup in DrupalApplication and also corrects how RecipeTestTrait invokes dr so that it works correctly in Composer-based installs (such as drupal/core-recommended) rather than only in raw core checkouts.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

In DrupalApplication::bootstrap(), the site path resolution now checks getenv('DRUPAL_DEV_SITE_PATH') first and falls back to DrupalKernel::findSitePath() only when the env var is absent. This mirrors the behaviour that existed in the now-deprecated BootableCommandTrait::getSitePath().

RecipeTestTrait::runDrupalCommand() received a related fix: it previously invoked core/scripts/dr relative to $this->root (the core root). It now uses vendor/bin/dr relative to the Composer project root, retrieved via Composer\InstalledVersions::getRootPackage()['install_path']. This ensures the correct class loader is in place and makes the trait work in installations where core lives under vendor/drupal/core rather than at the repository root.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 11, 2026
  • Committed: June 18, 2026 (7 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • dww (3281d Consulting)
  • phenaproxima (Acquia)
  • moshe
  • mradcliffe (Kosada)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

phenaproxima reported the breakage in Slack (they were the original author of BootableCommandTrait and noticed the missing env-var support), then contributed a commit to the MR fixing RecipeTestTrait::runDrupalCommand() to use vendor/bin/dr. dww opened the issue and authored the core DrupalApplication fix, then reviewed phenaproxima's commit. The two effectively reviewed each other's work before marking the MR RTBC. catch merged it urgently because the regression was blocking Drupal CMS testing.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601433: Fix local_status deprecation by clearing source in a post update
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +9/−0 · php +9
  • Diff: 7d0ebcd
  • Discussion: #3601433 · 4 contributors · 14 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

Multilingual sites upgrading from Drupal 11.3 to 11.4 could encounter errors in the translation update workflow because locale translation source data stored by existing sites (as plain stdClass objects) was no longer compatible with the LocaleTranslationSource class introduced by the deprecation work in #3590050. A new post-update hook clears that stale source data automatically during the database update process, and the deprecated locale_translation_get_status() shim is fixed to load its required include file before delegating to the new service.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The deprecation of locale_translation_get_status() (see #3590050) moved locale source tracking to the LocaleSource service, which now returns LocaleTranslationSource objects. Sites that had already run a translation check stored plain stdClass objects for locale project data in the database. After upgrading to 11.4 that stored data was loaded back as stdClass but code expected LocaleTranslationSource, causing failures.

The fix adds locale_post_update_clear_sources() in locale.post_update.php, which calls \Drupal::service(LocaleSource::class)->clearSources(). Because this source information is non-persistent (it is rebuilt automatically when stale), clearing it on upgrade is safe and sufficient.

A second bug was also fixed in the deprecated locale_translation_get_status() shim in locale.module: the old function loaded locale.translation.inc as a side effect, but the new shim did not. Tools such as Drush's locale:update command relied on that side effect to make _locale_translation_default_update_options() available. The fix adds an explicit \Drupal::moduleHandler()->loadInclude('locale', 'inc', 'locale.translation') call before the service delegation.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 16, 2026
  • Committed: June 18, 2026 (2 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3590350: WorkspacePublisher doesn't roll back when a PHP Error is thrown during publishing
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +95/−8 · php +95/−8
  • Diff: f239996
  • Discussion: #3590350 · 4 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

Sites using the Workspaces module were exposed to a data-integrity risk: if a PHP \Error subclass (such as \TypeError or \AssertionError) was thrown during a workspace publish or merge operation, the database transaction was not rolled back. This meant the first entities in the save loop could be committed to Live while later ones were not, leaving content in a partially published state with no error logged on the workspaces channel. The fix ensures the transaction is always rolled back and the throwable always logged, regardless of whether an \Exception or an \Error subclass escapes the save loop.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Both WorkspacePublisher::publish() and WorkspaceMerger::merge() wrapped their entity-save loops in try / catch (\Exception $e) blocks that called $transaction->rollBack() and Error::logException() before rethrowing. Because PHP's \Error hierarchy is not a subclass of \Exception, any \Error thrown from inside the closure (for example from a hook_entity_presave() implementation or a downstream service called during $entity->save()) bypassed the catch block entirely. The fix widens both catch clauses to catch (\Throwable $e). Error::logException() already accepts \Throwable, so no change to the catch body was required. A PHPStan suppression for a false-positive isset.variable warning in WorkspaceMerger.php was also removed as it became unnecessary. A kernel test (WorkspacePublisherTest::testPublishThrowableRollback) was added using a #[Hook('node_presave')] implementation on the test class itself to simulate a failure mid-loop; a data provider covers both \RuntimeException (previously caught) and \Error (previously missed), asserting that neither node's Live revision changes and that the error is recorded on the workspaces logger channel.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 15, 2026
  • Committed: June 15, 2026 (1 month and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • amateescu
  • godotislate

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3524377: Allow to skip OOP hooks and services for modules that are not installed
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 9 files, +206/−6 · php +201/−6 · yml +5
  • Diff: 11e9b82
  • Discussion: #3524377 · 11 contributors · 90 comments
  • Followers: 20

Summary

Drupal now provides a #[HookDependsOnModule] PHP attribute that module developers can place on an OOP hook class or method to make registration conditional on another module being installed. When the named module is absent, the annotated hook method (or entire class) is skipped at container build time. If every hook method in a class is skipped, the class is not added to the container at all. This solves two problems: it avoids unnecessary service registrations for hooks that can never fire, and it lets developers declare service-container dependencies as required constructor arguments instead of nullable ones, because the hook class will simply never be instantiated when the required module is absent.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New #[HookDependsOnModule] attribute for conditionally registering OOP hook classes or methods based on whether a module is installed.

Technical details

The new Drupal\Core\Hook\Attribute\HookDependsOnModule class is a PHP attribute declared with TARGET_CLASS | TARGET_METHOD | IS_REPEATABLE, accepts a single $module string, and implements HookAttributeInterface. The check is performed inside HookCollectorPass::collectModuleHookImplementations() against $this->modules, the list of installed modules already available in the pass. Class-level attributes are now also serialized into the file cache entry (as class_attributes) so the check is fast on warm runs.

The logic runs in two stages: first a loop over class-level HookDependsOnModule instances uses continue 2 to skip the entire file if any dependency is unmet; then a per-method inner loop does the same for method-level instances. A hook class with no surviving method registrations is never added to the container.

Two in-tree examples land in the same commit. FileViewsHooks receives #[HookDependsOnModule('views')] on the class, which lets the formerly nullable ?FieldViewsDataProvider constructor argument become required. MenuUiHooks::menuDelete() receives #[HookDependsOnModule('node')] on the method, removing a runtime $this->entityTypeManager->hasDefinition('node_type') guard that was previously needed to handle the case where node module was absent.

Upgrade

Module developers with OOP hook classes that inject services from an optionally-installed module can now declare those dependencies as required and add the attribute:

use Drupal\Core\Hook\Attribute\Hook;
use Drupal\Core\Hook\Attribute\HookDependsOnModule;

// Class-level: skip the entire class when 'search' is not installed.
#[HookDependsOnModule('search')]
class NodeSearchHooks {
  public function __construct(
    protected readonly SearchIndexInterface $searchIndex, // now required, not nullable
  ) {}

  #[Hook('cron')]
  public function cron(): void { ... }
}

// Method-level: skip only this method when 'node' is not installed.
#[Hook('menu_delete')]
#[HookDependsOnModule('node')]
public function menuDelete(EntityInterface $entity): void { ... }

The attribute is repeatable; if any named module is absent the class or method is skipped. Because a non-existent attribute class is not a fatal error (unlike a non-existent named parameter on an existing attribute), contrib modules can safely use #[HookDependsOnModule] and simply declare a minimum core version requirement for the class to be recognized.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 14, 2025
  • Committed: June 15, 2026 (1 year and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • kristiaanvandeneynde (Factorial.io)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • acbramley (PreviousNext)
  • godotislate

Collaboration

berdir opened the issue and drove the implementation. A key design debate in the thread concerned whether to add a named parameter to the existing #[Hook] attribute (or a generic ConditionalRegister attribute) versus introducing a dedicated class. berdir's argument, confirmed with a live code snippet at https://3v4l.org/31a3W, was that using a non-existent named parameter on an existing attribute is a PHP fatal error, making a separate attribute class the only approach that is safe across the multi-version contrib compatibility window. kristiaanvandeneynde pushed back in favor of a more generic name to leave room for future dependency types (themes, callbacks), and berdir and nicxvan countered that purpose-specific attribute classes are already precedent in the hook system and that any future use case can get its own attribute without BC concerns. acbramley did most of the rebase and cleanup work in the final stretch, renaming the constructor parameter to $module for consistency with #[Hook] and applying godotislate's doc-review suggestions before the commit landed.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3400181: [regression] calling TypedConfigManager::getDefinition() causes cache pollution
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +3/−22 · php +3/−22
  • Diff: 39c1792
  • Discussion: #3400181 · 7 contributors · 21 comments
  • Followers: 13

Summary

A regression in TypedConfigManager caused config schema definitions to be stored incorrectly in the in-memory cache when the double-bracket (||) composite type syntax was used. Once the cache was polluted by a call to getDefinition(), subsequent typed config lookups for the same type could resolve against the wrong (incomplete) definition, leading to silent schema mismatches. This bug was severe enough that at least one contributed module (Canvas) had to override the config.typed service entirely to work around it.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause was introduced by #2663410. TypedConfigManager::getDefinitionWithReplacements() handles a special edge case where a schema type uses the || separator (e.g. wrapping.test.double_brackets.*||test.double_brackets.cat.dog). When this edge case fires, the method processes the composite type and then unconditionally wrote the result to $this->definitions[$type]. The problem: getDefinition() calls getDefinitionWithReplacements() without passing any $replacements, so this unconditional write stored an un-replaced, incomplete definition in the cache. Any subsequent call that did pass replacements would then hit the stale cache entry and skip the replacement logic entirely.

The fix in TypedConfigManager.php adds a single guard: if (!empty($replacements)) { $this->definitions[$type] = $definition; }. Only calls that actually performed replacements are now allowed to populate the cache entry. Three clearCachedDefinitions() workaround calls in ConfigSchemaTest::testConfigSaveWithWrappingSchemaDoubleBrackets(), each accompanied by a @todo Remove in #3400181 annotation, are removed as a direct consequence.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: November 8, 2023
  • Committed: June 14, 2026 (2 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • claudiu.cristea (Webikon)
  • huzooka (Munyu)
  • dimilias (European Commission and European Union Institutions, Agencies and Bodies)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • herved (European Commission and European Union Institutions, Agencies and Bodies)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

wim leers filed the issue in November 2023 and identified both the regressing commit (#2663410) and the exact code path responsible. Over a year and a half later, huzooka submitted the MR after the Canvas project (#3568264) ran into the bug in a real-world schema and had to override the core service as a workaround. huzooka's comment in the MR noted that removing the existing clearCachedDefinitions() calls from the test was sufficient test coverage, a point claudiu.cristea seconded, sparing the need for new test authorship.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3408420: Deleting a workflow can lead to the deletion of unaffected views
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3408420 · 4 contributors · 17 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

Deleting a workflow could silently delete views and other configuration entities that had no real dependency on the workflow. The same underlying defect could also cause a user role to lose a permission when an unrelated field was removed from a content type with Layout Builder enabled. Both symptoms were caused by a dependency-ordering bug in the configuration system that made config entities get processed in the wrong sequence during a cascading deletion. The fix ensures that the dependency processing order is preserved correctly, so only configuration that genuinely depends on the deleted item is affected.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The defect was in ConfigManager::findConfigEntityDependenciesAsEntities(). That method calls findConfigEntityDependencies() to get an ordered list of config names (ordered so that dependents come before their dependencies), then loads the corresponding entities using EntityStorageInterface::loadMultiple() grouped by entity type. The problem: loadMultiple() returns entities sorted by their own storage order, not by the dependency order. This silently reordered the returned entity list. getConfigEntitiesToChangeOnDependencyRemoval() processes the list from the end, relying on the ordering to ensure a dependency is processed before any entity that depends on it. When the order was wrong, a role could be processed before an entity view display it depended on. Because that display appeared to be in the removal list, the system incorrectly stripped the role's permission.

The fix pre-populates the result array with keys in the correct dependency order using array_fill_keys(array_keys($dependencies), NULL), then fills in each loaded entity by its config dependency name ($loaded_entity->getConfigDependencyName()). A final array_filter() removes any entries that were not loaded. This preserves the order from findConfigEntityDependencies() regardless of how loadMultiple() sorts entities internally.

The fix was applied in the related issue #3504368 and committed to 10.5.x, 11.1.x, and 11.x. Issue #3408420 was subsequently closed because the same ordering fix resolved the workflow-deletion-deletes-views scenario.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: December 13, 2023

Collaboration

The root cause proved elusive: the alphabetical name of a contributing module changed which config objects were in the static cache on first load versus subsequent loads, altering the order findConfigEntityDependencies() returned, which meant the bug was not reliably reproducible in a clean install. godotislate isolated the mechanism by tracing the list order through findConfigEntityDependenciesAsEntities() and identifying how grouping by entity type for loadMultiple() destroyed the dependency order. godotislate then proposed and implemented the fix in MR 11141, while dpagini confirmed the fix resolved the original project issue.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3599842: guzzlehttp/psr7 needs to be updated to >2.10.2 to fix 2 security issues
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 10.6.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +13/−12 · lock +11/−10 · json +2/−2
  • Diff: 833a440
  • Discussion: #3599842 · 6 contributors · 24 comments
  • Followers: 22

Summary

Two medium-severity security vulnerabilities in the guzzlehttp/psr7 library (CVE-2026-48998, Host Confusion via Authority Reinterpretation; and CVE-2026-49214, CRLF Injection via URI Host Component) were publicly registered on June 11 2026 against versions below 2.10.2. Drupal core's drupal/core-recommended package was pinning ~2.8.0, which prevented composer update from pulling in a safe release and caused composer audit to fail on any project using that metapackage. This change raises the minimum to ^2.10.2 (resolved to 2.10.4 in the lock file) across the affected branches, so sites can install a clean dependency tree without workarounds.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Security: Two medium-severity CVEs in guzzlehttp/psr7 (<2.10.2): CVE-2026-48998 (Host Confusion via Authority Reinterpretation) and CVE-2026-49214 (CRLF Injection via URI Host Component).
  • Requirements: Minimum guzzlehttp/psr7 raised from ~2.8.0 to ~2.10.4 in drupal/core-recommended.

Technical details

The constraint for guzzlehttp/psr7 was updated in three places within the single commit on main (with parallel commits on 11.3.x and 10.6.x): composer.json at the repo root changed from ^2.8.0 to ^2.10.2, core/composer.json (used by drupal/core-recommended) changed its pinned tilde constraint from ~2.8.0 to ~2.10.4, and composer.lock was regenerated to record guzzlehttp/psr7 at 2.10.4. The 11.4.x and 11.x branches were already on 2.10.4 and needed no changes. The contributor noted that bumping to 2.11.0 introduced deprecations, so 2.10.4 was chosen as the target instead. The two CVEs affect URI parsing: GHSA-hq7v-mx3g-29hw covers CRLF injection via the URI host component, and GHSA-34xg-wgjx-8xph covers host confusion via authority reinterpretation.

Upgrade

Sites running drupal/recommended-project on affected branches (main, 11.3.x, 10.6.x) should update to the patched Drupal core release and run composer update. Before the patched release is available, the workaround is:

composer require "guzzlehttp/psr7":"2.10.4 as 2.8.1"

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 12, 2026
  • Committed: June 13, 2026

Key contributors

  • deviantintegral
  • godotislate
  • dww (3281d Consulting)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585891: Deprecate Validating CSRF tokens with the 'rest' key in CsrfRequestHeaderAccessCheck
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +9/−6 · php +9/−6
  • Diff: 41b8957
  • Discussion: #3585891 · 6 contributors · 25 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

Drupal 11.4 deprecates the fallback that allowed CSRF tokens validated against the old 'rest' key to pass the X-CSRF-Token access check. This key was a leftover from a Drupal 8 migration path and a @todo comment in the code targeted its removal as far back as Drupal 8.3. Any module that still generates or validates CSRF tokens using the literal string 'rest' will now trigger a deprecation notice; the behaviour continues working but will break in Drupal 12. The user.login.http endpoint was also updated to generate its CSRF token using the canonical key.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: CSRF token validation and generation using the hardcoded 'rest' key is deprecated in 11.4.0; switch callers to CsrfRequestHeaderAccessCheck::TOKEN_KEY before Drupal 12.0.0

Technical details

In CsrfRequestHeaderAccessCheck::access(), the old double-validate() call (checking both self::TOKEN_KEY and 'rest') has been restructured: if the primary check fails and the token validates under 'rest', a @trigger_error() deprecation is issued but the request is still allowed through. If neither key validates, AccessResult::forbidden() is returned as before. The constant TOKEN_KEY is 'X-CSRF-Token'.

UserAuthenticationController::login() previously called $this->csrfToken->get('rest') to produce the CSRF token included in the login response. It now imports CsrfRequestHeaderAccessCheck and calls $this->csrfToken->get(CsrfRequestHeaderAccessCheck::TOKEN_KEY). This was the concrete caller that caused initial test failures when a straight removal was attempted, and it motivated pivoting to a deprecation-first approach rather than an immediate removal.

Upgrade

Any code that calls $csrfToken->get('rest') to produce a CSRF token for REST requests must switch to the constant:

// Before (deprecated):
$token = $this->csrfToken->get('rest');

// After:
use Drupal\Core\Access\CsrfRequestHeaderAccessCheck;
$token = $this->csrfToken->get(CsrfRequestHeaderAccessCheck::TOKEN_KEY);

Similarly, any explicit $csrfToken->validate($value, 'rest') calls should use CsrfRequestHeaderAccessCheck::TOKEN_KEY as the second argument. The 'rest' key fallback is removed in Drupal 12.0.0.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 20, 2026
  • Committed: June 12, 2026 (1 month and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • sourav_paul (Innoraft)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • macsim
  • godotislate
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

Collaboration

prudloff noted in the thread that tests were failing when the removal was attempted outright because UserAuthenticationController was still producing tokens with the 'rest' key, and that contrib modules could be doing the same. This observation prompted the switch from direct removal to a formal deprecation cycle.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3593963: Field loading can hit the MySQL 61 table join limit if there are ~60 fields
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +320/−100 · php +320/−100
  • Diff: 7d3b034
  • Discussion: #3593963 · 7 contributors · 41 comments
  • Followers: 15

Summary

Sites with content types that define roughly 60 or more fields can hit a fatal database error when loading entities, because MySQL and MariaDB enforce a hard limit of 61 tables per JOIN and SQLite has a comparable limit. The error surfaced in Drupal 11.4.x after earlier optimizations consolidated field loading into single queries that JOIN each dedicated field storage table. After this fix, entity loading on sites with large numbers of fields works correctly again regardless of how many fields are defined.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The problem was introduced by #3564689 and #3551308, which changed SqlContentEntityStorage::loadFromDedicatedTables() to load all single-cardinality fields in one query and all multiple-cardinality fields in another, each using a LEFT JOIN per field. With ~60 or more fields that use dedicated storage tables, the number of joins exceeds MySQL's documented limit of 61.

The fix adds a constant FIELD_MINIMUM_CHUNK_SIZE = 25 to SqlContentEntityStorage and splits both the single-cardinality and multiple-cardinality field lists into chunks before querying. The chunking logic merges the last chunk into the penultimate one, so 26 fields load as a single chunk of 26 (not 25 + 1) and 51 fields load as chunks of 25 and 26. This keeps each query well under database limits while avoiding an excessively small final batch.

The inline field-loading code was extracted into two new private methods, loadSingleCardinalityFields() and loadMultipleCardinalityFields(), which are called in a foreach loop over the chunks. A $load_shared_table_fields boolean is passed to loadSingleCardinalityFields() and is only true for the first chunk, preventing shared base/revision-data-table fields from being fetched and overwritten on subsequent iterations. A new kernel test testEntityWithManyFieldsLoad() in SqlContentEntityStorageTest creates 71 fields on entity_test_rev and asserts all field values survive a save/load and save/loadRevision cycle for both single and unlimited cardinality.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 5, 2026
  • Committed: June 10, 2026 (4 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • godotislate
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • joachim (Chicken)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3590897: Sidebar toggle is visible and functioning in media library dialog, but shouldn't be there
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +1/−12 · php +1/−12
  • Diff: 05482f5
  • Discussion: #3590897 · 4 contributors · 16 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

When using the default admin theme and clicking the media browser on a media reference field, the sidebar toggle button was incorrectly rendered inside the media library dialog. This allowed users to trigger main-page navigation from within the overlay. The fix removes the spurious button from all AJAX-driven dialogs, including media library popups opened via media reference fields.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The problem was in FormHooks::stickyActionButtonsAndSidebar() in the default admin theme. The early-return guard that prevents injecting the sidebar toggle and sticky action button markup into dialogs used a private isModalOrOffcanvas() helper. That helper checked the _wrapper_format request parameter for drupal_modal or drupal_dialog substrings. However, the media library opened from a media reference field sends _wrapper_format=drupal_ajax, which does not match either string, so the guard never fired and the sidebar toggle was included in the form output.

The fix replaces the call to the now-deleted isModalOrOffcanvas() with a call to the existing isAjax() helper. Because the default admin theme does not support sticky action buttons in any AJAX context, the broader isAjax() guard is the correct check. The isModalOrOffcanvas() private method was removed entirely.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 19, 2026
  • Committed: June 10, 2026 (21 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • jurgenhaas (LakeDrops)
  • mherchel (Dripyard)
  • bernardm28

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3592497: Buttons are not rendered on the bottom of the page for Node create/edit for
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +4/−1 · twig +4/−1
  • Diff: a81450d
  • Discussion: #3592497 · 6 contributors · 19 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

On the node create and edit form, the Save and Preview action buttons were rendering immediately below the Title field instead of at the bottom of the page when using Drupal's new default admin theme (introduced in 11.x). This has been fixed; the buttons now appear at the bottom of the form as expected.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The template responsible for the node edit form layout in the new default admin theme was passing the wrong key names to the form|without(...) Twig filter. It excluded gin_actions and gin_sidebar_toggle, which are Gin-theme identifiers that do not exist in Drupal core's node form, so the actual actions element was never suppressed from the main region. It was therefore rendered inline, right after the title field.

The fix has two parts. First, the exclusion list in form|without(...) is corrected: gin_actions becomes actions and gin_sidebar_toggle becomes default_admin_sidebar_toggle. Second, a new <div class="layout-region layout-region-node-footer"> block is added after the secondary (sidebar) region and explicitly renders {{ form.actions }} there, placing the buttons at the bottom of the form where they belong.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 28, 2026
  • Committed: June 9, 2026 (12 days and 3 commits later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3590364: Part 2 of Consolidate, refactor, remove and untangle Claro's CSS from Default Admin theme
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 92 files, +4828/−9917 · css +4793/−9798 · yml +28/−118 · svg +6 · js +1/−1
  • Diff: 3056dde
  • Discussion: #3590364 · 8 contributors · 68 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

This issue completes the second phase of CSS consolidation for the new default_admin admin theme (see #3582833 for Part 1). The remaining migration CSS files inherited from Gin and Claro have been merged into the theme's permanent stylesheet locations and the migration directory stubs deleted. Along the way, several visual improvements and bug fixes landed: rounded corner radii are now sharper (--base-border-radius changed from 0.5rem to 2px), the header background shifts to a bluer tone, media library and dialog layouts were repaired, and dark-mode support was added or fixed in several components. The autocomplete loading indicator was redesigned from a background-image SVG to a CSS animation spinner. Any site using the default_admin theme will see these visual changes automatically after updating to 11.4.

Impact

Affects front-end developers.

  • Theme change: .claro-autocomplete renamed to .gin-autocomplete; .project__action_button dropped from .button rule; --base-border-radius changed from 0.5rem to 2px; .button now uses display: inline-flex instead of inline-block

Technical details

The migration directory core/themes/default_admin/migration/css/ held Gin-origin stylesheet stubs for components, layout, and theme overrides. This issue merged all remaining stubs into the canonical css/ tree and removed the stubs.

Key mechanical changes visible in the diff: --base-border-radius was corrected from 0.5rem to 2px and --gin-bg-header changed from #eeeff3 to #e1eafc in variables.pcss.css. --vertical-tabs-border-color was fixed to reference --gin-border-color instead of the nonexistent --details-border-color. The .button rule switched from display: inline-block to display: inline-flex; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box, and the .project__action_button selector was dropped from that rule entirely. In autocomplete-loading.module.pcss.css, the Claro-origin wrapper class .claro-autocomplete and its sibling .claro-autocomplete__message were renamed to .gin-autocomplete and .gin-autocomplete__message; the SVG spinner background-image was replaced with a CSS @keyframes gin-throbber animation. The CKEditor 5 integration file, which previously contained only a single --ck-color-base-border override, now maps the full suite of --ck-* custom properties to --gin-* design tokens. New standalone files were created for components that had no Claro equivalent: description-toggle, contextual-links, edit-form, revisions, settings, sidebar, and status. Details and dialog components gained dark-mode rules and logical-property rewrites. Toolbar integration CSS was removed because the toolbar module is being deprecated for Drupal 12.

Upgrade

Contrib modules or custom CSS that target .claro-autocomplete or .claro-autocomplete__message must be updated:

/* Before */
.claro-autocomplete { … }
.claro-autocomplete__message { … }

/* After */
.gin-autocomplete { … }
.gin-autocomplete__message { … }

The .project__action_button selector no longer inherits .button styles from default_admin. If a module relied on this for button appearance in the admin theme, add the .button class directly to the element or target .project__action_button explicitly in your own stylesheet.

Custom CSS that references --base-border-radius expecting 0.5rem rounded corners will now receive 2px. Override the variable in your sub-theme if the old value is required.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 15, 2026
  • Committed: June 9, 2026 (25 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • mherchel (Dripyard)
  • bernardm28
  • dharizza (Evolving Web)
  • mukeysh (gai Technologies Pvt Ltd)
  • jurgenhaas (LakeDrops)

Collaboration

mherchel drove the bulk of the work and documented design decisions as they went: noting that dialog CSS contained an entire copy of form styles that could be removed, catching that the autocomplete_deluxe contrib module had bespoke overrides baked into core (opening a separate issue), and flagging that mukeysh's first contributions accidentally committed plain CSS instead of PCSS and broke image paths. dharizza completed maintenance-page, breadcrumb, sidebar, node-preview, tableselect, navigation, and top-bar. bernardm28 contributed status component work and provided Safari-specific QA feedback that identified two rendering issues, one of which was already being tracked at #3590897. jurgenhaas performed an independent full manual review of all admin pages against both the unpatched branch and Gin 5 on Drupal 11, confirmed no regressions, and documented remaining visual differences in a separate tracking issue.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3590050: Deprecate and replace locale_status related functions
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 18 files, +453/−313 · php +453/−313
  • Diff: f6c6aec
  • Discussion: #3590050 · 4 contributors · 37 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

Five procedural functions in the locale module that managed translation status have been deprecated in Drupal 11.4.0 and will be removed in 13.0.0. locale_translation_get_status, locale_translation_status_save, locale_translation_status_delete_languages, locale_translation_status_delete_projects, and locale_translation_clear_status are replaced by equivalent methods on the LocaleSource service. Custom modules or install profiles that call any of these functions must update before upgrading to Drupal 13.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: Five locale procedural functions deprecated in 11.4.0 and removed in 13.0.0: locale_translation_get_status, locale_translation_status_save, locale_translation_status_delete_languages, locale_translation_status_delete_projects, and locale_translation_clear_status replaced by methods on the LocaleSource service.
  • New API: New LocaleTranslationSource value object class and new methods loadSources(), loadSource(), saveSource(), deleteSources(), deleteSourcesByLanguage(), clearSources(), updateLastChecked(), and getLastChecked() added to the LocaleSource service.

Technical details

The LocaleSource service gained new public methods: loadSources(), loadSource(), saveSource(), deleteSources(), deleteSourcesByLanguage(), clearSources(), updateLastChecked(), and getLastChecked(). The key efficiency gain is that loadSources() now calls getMultiple() on the locale.translation_status key-value store for only the requested projects, rather than the unconditional loadAll() that the old locale_translation_get_status() always performed. Batch operations in LocaleFetch and LocaleProjectChecker were updated to call loadSource() per project/langcode, so each batch item touches only its own data.

A new typed value object LocaleTranslationSource (in Drupal\locale) replaces the anonymous stdClass that sourceBuild() previously returned. It carries the same public properties (project, langcode, type, timestamp, hash, last_checked, files, version, server_pattern, core, name) and is instantiated via LocaleTranslationSource::fromProject(). The last_checked timestamp previously stored in \Drupal::state() under locale.translation_last_checked is now stored inside the locale.translation_status key-value collection under the key last-checked, accessed only through LocaleSource::getLastChecked() and updateLastChecked().

The installer was further simplified in the second commit: _install_prepare_import() was removed entirely, and install_import_translations() now delegates directly to \Drupal::service(LocaleFetch::class)->buildUpdateBatch(). Installer test fixtures that previously hardcoded drupal-8.0.0.* translation filenames now use \Drupal::VERSION so the filename matches the running codebase, removing an entire category of version-matching logic that _install_prepare_import() had handled as a special case. locale_translation_build_sources() deprecation text was also corrected to point to sourceBuild() rather than the removed buildSources() method.

Upgrade

Replace the deprecated procedural calls with the LocaleSource service:

use Drupal\locale\LocaleSource;

$localeSource = \Drupal::service(LocaleSource::class);

// locale_translation_get_status($projects, $langcodes)
$sources = $localeSource->loadSources($projects, $langcodes);

// locale_translation_status_save($project, $langcode, $type, $data)
$localeSource->saveSource($project, $langcode, $type, $data);

// locale_translation_status_delete_languages($langcodes)
foreach ($langcodes as $langcode) {
  $localeSource->deleteSourcesByLanguage($langcode);
}

// locale_translation_status_delete_projects($projects)
$localeSource->deleteSources($projects);

// locale_translation_clear_status()
$localeSource->clearSources();

The return type of loadSources() is LocaleTranslationSource[][] rather than stdClass[][]. Code that previously assigned properties directly to the returned objects via $status[$project][$langcode]->type = 'current' continues to work because LocaleTranslationSource uses public typed properties, but the class must be imported.

If you previously read the last-checked timestamp directly from state (\Drupal::state()->get('locale.translation_last_checked')), use $localeSource->getLastChecked() instead. That state key is no longer written by core.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 14, 2026
  • First commit: June 4, 2026 (21 days later)
  • Last commit: June 9, 2026 (5 days and 4 commits later)

Key contributors

  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

nicxvan and berdir collaborated closely on this issue. berdir ported analysis notes from the MR thread identifying the layered inefficiency: every call to locale_translation_get_status() loaded all project data from key-value regardless of which project was needed, and callers then called buildSources() again inside loops, producing redundant work. nicxvan uncovered the tricky installer failure caused by _install_prepare_import() relying on version-string side effects: \Drupal::service(LocaleProjectRepository::class)->set($project) was being called with the version extracted from the .po filename (e.g. 8.0.0), implicitly updating the project version so subsequent lookups matched. nicxvan diagnosed this by manually tracing the installer steps and confirmed in comment 21 that explicitly setting the file URI and filename on the loaded source and calling saveSource() was the correct replacement. berdir summarized in comment 32 that the remaining test failures on the forward-port were due to tests still constructing filenames with the old major-version pattern, and opened follow-up issues for further cleanup.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3593281: Rename "promoted to front page" to "promoted"
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 41 files, +61/−61 · php +32/−32 · yml +17/−17 · twig +12/−12
  • Diff: 234a5ae
  • Discussion: #3593281 · 4 contributors · 19 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

The long-standing "Promoted to front page" label on nodes has been renamed to simply "Promoted" across the entire admin UI. The old name was misleading because the /node listing is not the front page on most sites and, per the related initiative (#3572350), will no longer be enabled by default. Site editors will see "Promoted" in the publishing options when creating or editing content, "Promote content" and "Unpromote content" in bulk-action menus, and updated descriptions in the Frontpage view. No database schema, field machine names, or PHP class/method names changed.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Config change: Labels in system.action.node_promote_action.yml, system.action.node_unpromote_action.yml, views.view.frontpage.yml, and field.field.node.*.promote.yml default config updated to use the new terminology.

Technical details

The diff touches labels and human-readable strings only; the underlying promote field, NodeInterface::PROMOTED/NOT_PROMOTED constants, and all method names (isPromoted(), etc.) are unchanged. Updated locations include: the base field definition label in Node::baseFieldDefinitions(), the NodeTypeForm publishing-options checkbox, the #[Action] annotations on PromoteNode and DemoteNode (and their corresponding system.action.node_promote_action.yml / node_unpromote_action.yml config files), Views data strings in NodeViewsData::getViewsData(), the views.view.frontpage.yml description and empty-state text, field.field.node.article.promote.yml and field.field.node.recipe.promote.yml default config, the get-started.html.twig onboarding template, node.module help text, and node.html.twig docblock comments in all core themes. Config schema labels in node.schema.yml for action.configuration.node_unpromote_action and action.configuration.node_promote_action are also updated. Because many of the renamed strings are translatable, existing interface translations (on localize.drupal.org or in custom translation files) for the old strings will no longer match and will fall back to English until updated. The full list of changed strings was documented in a change record at https://www.drupal.org/node/3593641.

Upgrade

No code changes are required. If your site has exported configuration that includes any of the affected default config files (e.g. system.action.node_promote_action.yml, system.action.node_unpromote_action.yml, views.view.frontpage.yml, or field.field.node.*.promote.yml), re-export your config after updating to pick up the new labels and avoid config-hash mismatches. If you have custom interface translations for any of the renamed strings, update them to match the new source strings listed in the change record.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 2, 2026
  • Committed: June 9, 2026 (6 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)
  • macsim
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

Reviewer macsim noted that multiple translatable strings had been renamed and that a Change Record was needed so translation teams on localize.drupal.org would be aware of the fallback risk. gábor hojtsy then created the change record (https://www.drupal.org/node/3593641) based on the list macsim compiled in the thread.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3453474: CLI entry point in Drupal Core
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 45 files, +1439/−150 · php +1394/−144 · yml +31/−1 · lock +4/−1 · json +4/−1 · dist +3 · txt +2/−2 · sh +1/−1
  • Diff: 21e3ddf
  • Discussion: #3453474 · 28 contributors · 145 comments
  • Followers: 86

Summary

Drupal core now ships a fully-featured CLI tool available as vendor/bin/dr. Out of the box it provides cache:rebuild (alias cr), system:cron (alias cron), system:status, recipe:apply, recipe:info, and content:export commands, covering the most common tasks previously requiring Drush. Module developers can add their own commands by placing a class in their module's src/Command/ directory and annotating it with #[AsCommand]; no service YAML registration is needed. The legacy core/scripts/drupal entry point still functions, and vendor/bin/dr install / vendor/bin/dr quick-start replace the old php core/scripts/drupal install invocations documented in INSTALL.md and CI scripts.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: BootableCommandTrait::boot() deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0; the new CLI bootstraps Drupal automatically.
  • New API: New vendor/bin/dr CLI entry point backed by DrupalApplication; modules auto-discovered via ConsoleCompilerPass scanning src/Command/ for #[AsCommand]; built-in commands cache:rebuild, system:cron, system:status, recipe:apply, recipe:info, and content:export added.

Technical details

The binary is registered in core's composer.json as scripts/dr, which makes it available as vendor/bin/dr after composer install. The script returns a Symfony Runtime application, which is now an explicit non-dev dependency (symfony/runtime ^8.0).

DrupalApplication extends Symfony's Application and provides lazy bootstrap: on first command lookup it instantiates DrupalKernel with $allow_dumping=FALSE (to avoid reusing a cached container), boots the site, pushes a request onto the stack, and then loads module-contributed commands via DrupalApplication::addDrupalCommands(). A global --url option (also readable from the DRUPAL_URI environment variable) controls which site is selected in multi-site setups. Commands that do not need a running site (e.g. InstallCommand, CacheRebuildCommand) are registered as default commands and run without bootstrapping.

ConsoleCompilerPass (a new @internal compiler pass) runs at container compile time. It iterates container.namespaces, looks inside each module's src/Command/ directory recursively, and registers any class bearing #[AsCommand] as an autowired, public service tagged console.command. It also checks the full class hierarchy to skip commands that extend optional third-party base classes (such as Drush commands) whose parents may not be installed. Symfony's own AddConsoleCommandPass then processes the tagged services into a lazy CommandLoaderInterface (console.command_loader). Commands that set their name only via configure() (without the attribute) are not auto-discovered and must be declared explicitly in a module's *.services.yml with { name: console.command }.

DrupalConsoleLogger (a PSR-3 logger) and ConsoleSubscriber (a ConsoleEvents::COMMAND listener) together route Drupal's internal log messages to the console output during command execution, with notices shown at normal verbosity and info/debug messages gated behind -v/-vvv.

Several existing @internal commands were refactored as part of this change: ContentExportCommand moved from Drupal\Core\DefaultContent to Drupal\Core\DefaultContent\Command; RecipeCommand and RecipeInfoCommand moved from Drupal\Core\Recipe to Drupal\Core\Recipe\Command. All three dropped BootableCommandTrait in favor of constructor-injected services, since DrupalApplication now handles bootstrapping automatically. QuickStartCommand was rewritten as an invokable class using #[Argument] and #[Option] attributes instead of extending Command. DrupalKernel::compileContainer() was updated to also scan components that expose a Command/ directory, ensuring core-level commands are discovered.

The full developer API is documented in a new @defgroup console_commands section in module.api.php, including both the Command-extending style and the lighter invokable-class style. Related bootstrap work on Symfony Runtime was tracked in #3313404.

Upgrade

Replace php core/scripts/drupal invocations with vendor/bin/dr:

# Before
php core/scripts/drupal install standard
php core/scripts/drupal quick-start --help

# After
vendor/bin/dr install standard
vendor/bin/dr quick-start --help

Commands that use BootableCommandTrait::boot() must be rewritten to receive their dependencies via constructor injection; DrupalApplication now boots Drupal automatically before execute() is called:

// Before
class MyCommand extends Command {
  use BootableCommandTrait;
  public function __construct($class_loader) {
    parent::__construct('my:command');
    $this->classLoader = $class_loader;
  }
  protected function execute(InputInterface $input, OutputInterface $output): int {
    $container = $this->boot()->getContainer();
    $service = $container->get(MyService::class);
    // ...
  }
}

// After — place in src/Command/MyCommand.php
#[AsCommand(name: 'my:command', description: 'My command.')]
class MyCommand extends Command {
  public function __construct(protected readonly MyService $service) {
    parent::__construct();
  }
  protected function execute(InputInterface $input, OutputInterface $output): int {
    // $this->service is already available; no manual bootstrap needed.
  }
}

Code that references the now-moved @internal classes by their old fully-qualified names must update the namespace:

| Old | New | |---|---| | Drupal\Core\DefaultContent\ContentExportCommand | Drupal\Core\DefaultContent\Command\ContentExportCommand | | Drupal\Core\Recipe\RecipeCommand | Drupal\Core\Recipe\Command\RecipeCommand | | Drupal\Core\Recipe\RecipeInfoCommand | Drupal\Core\Recipe\Command\RecipeInfoCommand |

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 9, 2024
  • Committed: June 8, 2026 (1 year and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • dpi (PreviousNext)
  • chi
  • andypost (Skilld)
  • mradcliffe (Kosada)
  • acbramley (PreviousNext)
  • aaronmchale (The University of Edinburgh)
  • bhanu951
  • greg.1.anderson (Pantheon)
  • moshe weitzman
  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)
  • bircher (Nuvole)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • franksj
  • rymo
  • godotislate
  • rfay (DDEV)
  • xjm (Zoocha)
  • dww (3281d Consulting)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • arrow
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • kingdutch (Open Social)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3132725: "Limit list to selected items" on exposed filters does not filter
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +43/−2 · php +43/−2
  • Diff: f7c6fbe
  • Discussion: #3132725 · 11 contributors · 51 comments
  • Followers: 13

Summary

The "Limit list to selected items" checkbox on exposed in_operator Views filters (such as Content type) has never functioned correctly. When that option was checked and the exposed filter's default "- Any -" selection was in effect, the result set was not limited to nodes matching the pre-selected filter values. The fix makes the option work as advertised: with "Limit list to selected items" enabled and "- Any -" selected, only records matching the configured filter values are returned.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause was a key-name mismatch in InOperator::acceptExposedInput(). The method guarded its "return TRUE when 'All' is selected and reduce is active" code path with !empty($this->options['expose']['limit']), but the expose form stores this option under the key 'reduce' (set in buildExposeForm() as $form['expose']['reduce']). Because no key named 'limit' ever exists in the expose options, the condition always evaluated to false and the block was never entered, leaving "All" input unhandled by the reduce logic. The fix is a one-character key rename from 'limit' to 'reduce' in that condition. A kernel test was added to FilterInOperatorTest::testFilterExposedReduce() that configures the age filter on the test_view with reduce => TRUE and value restricted to two ages, then asserts the result set contains only matching rows.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 30, 2020
  • Committed: June 8, 2026 (6 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • devad
  • dww
  • steyep
  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • carlos romero (hiberus)
  • dcam
  • godotislate

Collaboration

steyep identified the exact mismatch in a 2022 comment, pointing to the 'reduce' key in the form definition versus 'limit' in acceptExposedInput(), and provided a hook_views_pre_build() workaround. That analysis directly informed the eventual one-line fix. contributor dww, who noted he was independently affected by the bug, confirmed the MR worked and prompted the test to be converted from a unit test to a kernel test by pointing to FilterInOperatorTest as the right home. smustgrave carried out the conversion, and dww ran it locally to verify the failure-then-pass behavior before marking it RTBC.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3594092: loadUnchanged() returns an in-memory-modified entity when hook_entity_preload() swaps in a non-default revision
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +18/−0 · php +18
  • Diff: aad4502
  • Discussion: #3594092 · 3 contributors · 11 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

ContentEntityStorageBase::loadUnchanged() could return an entity with unsaved in-memory modifications instead of the persisted state when a module such as Workspaces swaps in a non-default revision via hook_entity_preload(). This is now fixed so the method always reflects the saved state of the entity.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

loadUnchanged() clears the entity-keyed static cache via parent::resetCache(), but a second static cache stores revisions keyed by {entityTypeId}:{id}:revisions. When hook_entity_preload() returns a non-default revision, the reload path goes through loadMultipleRevisions() to getFromStaticRevisionCache(), which still held the in-memory-modified object.

The fix adds a call to $this->memoryCache->invalidateTags([$revision_cache_tag]) inside loadUnchanged(), using the tag "{$this->entityTypeId}:{$id}:revisions", guarded by a check for isStaticallyCacheable() && isRevisionable(). This evicts the revision from the memory cache without touching the persistent cache, so the subsequent reload still benefits from the fast persistent-cache path rather than hitting storage unnecessarily. A new assertion in ContentEntityStorageBaseTest::testNonDefaultRevision() covers the scenario directly.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 7, 2026
  • Committed: June 8, 2026

Key contributors

  • amateescu
  • godotislate
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3592577: Ensure that hook attributes are never parsed from a stale opcache
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +14/−0 · php +14
  • Diff: 16d92a4
  • Discussion: #3592577 · 4 contributors · 20 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

After deploying new code that changes OOP hook implementations (such as moving a hook to a different class), sites could end up with stale hook data in the key/value store. This happened because PHP's opcache can hold old file bytecode for up to opcache.revalidate_freq seconds (default: 2s). If a container cache rebuild started during that window, hook attribute parsing via reflection would read the old class definitions and persist them. Subsequent requests would use this stale hook data until another manual cache rebuild occurred. The fix ensures opcache is explicitly invalidated for each file before it is inspected for hook attributes during a rebuild.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The hook attribute collector classes (HookCollectorPass for modules, and the equivalent for themes) walk PHP source files and parse their #[Hook] attributes using reflection. When a file has no valid entry in the file cache, the collector falls through to live reflection. The fix adds a call to OpCodeCache::invalidate($filename) at the top of that file-cache-miss branch in both collectModuleHookImplementations() and collectThemeHookImplementations(). OpCodeCache::invalidate() calls PHP's opcache_invalidate() under the hood; that function performs an mtime check and drops the cached bytecode for the file if it has changed on disk. This brings the opcache inline with the filesystem before reflection runs, preventing the reflection APIs from seeing stale class definitions. The change is confined to the cache-miss path, so it adds no overhead when the file cache is warm.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 28, 2026
  • Committed: June 8, 2026 (10 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

larowlan reported a similar symptom on a client site (hooks going missing outside of deployments, fixed by drush cr), which prompted useful discussion about whether the same root cause was involved. After investigation with database dumps, larowlan concluded the issue on their end stemmed from bad cache contexts in custom code rather than stale opcache, narrowing the scope of this fix back to the deployment race condition described in the issue body.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3581056: Introduce a OneTimeAuthentication service and deprecate user_pass_rehash
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 13 files, +294/−90 · php +293/−90 · yml +1
  • Diff: a38f1d1
  • Discussion: #3581056 · 10 contributors · 43 comments
  • Followers: 12

Summary

Drupal 11.4 introduces a new \Drupal\user\OneTimeAuthentication service that consolidates all one-time login and account-cancellation URL generation into one place. The four procedural functions user_pass_rehash(), user_pass_reset_url(), user_cancel_url(), and user_mail_tokens() are deprecated and will be removed in Drupal 13.0.0. Custom and contrib code that calls any of those functions will start triggering deprecation notices and must be updated before the Drupal 13 upgrade.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: user_pass_rehash(), user_pass_reset_url(), user_cancel_url(), user_mail_tokens() deprecated in 11.4.0, removed in 13.0.0; UserController::validatePathParameters() and ServerCommand::getOneTimeLoginUrl() deprecated in 11.4.0, removed in 12.0.0
  • New API: New final service \Drupal\user\OneTimeAuthentication with generateHmac(), verifyHmac(), generateOneTimeLoginUrl(), and generateCancelConfirmUrl() methods

Technical details

The new final readonly class OneTimeAuthentication lives in core/modules/user/src/OneTimeAuthentication.php and is registered as an autowireable service (Drupal\user\OneTimeAuthentication: ~ in user.services.yml). It depends on TimeInterface and LanguageManagerInterface. Its public non-internal methods are generateHmac(), verifyHmac(), generateOneTimeLoginUrl(), and generateCancelConfirmUrl(); the tokens() method is marked @internal and serves as a drop-in callback for Token::replace().

The procedural functions in user.module now emit E_USER_DEPRECATED and delegate to the service. user_pass_reset_url() and user_cancel_url() are removed in 13.0.0; UserController::validatePathParameters() (a protected helper) is deprecated and removed in 12.0.0 because protected methods carry no BC promise. ServerCommand::getOneTimeLoginUrl() is also deprecated in 11.4.0 with no replacement and removed in 12.0.0; ServerCommand::start() now calls the service directly.

UserController gains OneTimeAuthentication as an optional constructor argument (defaulting to a service-container fallback with a deprecation notice when omitted), and its internal validatePathParameters() now delegates to verifyHmac().

A key behavioral difference: generateOneTimeLoginUrl() returns a \Drupal\Core\Url object, not a string. It accepts an $immediate bool parameter that selects the user.reset.login route (previously callers appended /login to the URL string). The old destination query-string pattern that was manually concatenated is now handled via $url->mergeOptions(['query' => ['destination' => ...]]) on the returned object.

Upgrade

Replace calls to deprecated functions with the new service methods. The service is typehintable as \Drupal\user\OneTimeAuthentication for constructor injection, or retrievable via \Drupal::service(OneTimeAuthentication::class).

// Before
$hash = user_pass_rehash($account, $timestamp);
$login_url = user_pass_reset_url($account, $options);         // returns string
$cancel_url = user_cancel_url($account, $options);            // returns string
// In token callbacks:
$token_options = ['callback' => 'user_mail_tokens', ...];
// Deprecated method on UserController:
$valid = $this->validatePathParameters($user, $timestamp, $hash, $timeout);

// After
use Drupal\user\OneTimeAuthentication;
$ota = \Drupal::service(OneTimeAuthentication::class);

$hash      = $ota->generateHmac($account, $timestamp);
$login_url = $ota->generateOneTimeLoginUrl($account, $options)->toString(); // returns Url, call toString()
$cancel_url = $ota->generateCancelConfirmUrl($account, $options)->toString();
// For immediate login (replaces appending '/login' to the URL):
$login_url = $ota->generateOneTimeLoginUrl($account, $options, immediate: TRUE)->toString();
// In token callbacks:
$token_options = ['callback' => $ota->tokens(...), ...];
// In UserController subclasses (if overriding validatePathParameters):
$valid = $this->oneTimeAuthentication->verifyHmac($user, $timestamp, $hash, $timeout);

Note: UserController::validatePathParameters() is removed in 12.0.0 (not 13.0.0), so subclasses overriding it must migrate sooner.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 23, 2026
  • Committed: June 8, 2026 (2 months and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • znerol
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • phenaproxima (Acquia)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • benjifisher (Harvard Web Publishing)
  • heddn (MTech, LLC)
  • godotislate
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

Collaboration

A substantive design debate ran through the thread on two points. First, berdir argued against adding a PHP interface to the service because the team does not want to support alternative implementations; this keeps it easier to add new methods without BC concerns. Second, whether to mark the class final triggered a lengthy discussion referencing a still-open policy issue (#3488476). alexpott reviewed the security-critical code paths (confirming hash_equals() was preserved) and concluded the class should ship as final to actively discourage extension; catch agreed for this specific case. nicxvan and benjifisher drove several rounds of review covering coding standards, the deprecation timeline for the protected validatePathParameters() method, and change-record completeness before the committer's sign-off.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588490: Don't reset the extension lists in system requirements
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +13/−7 · php +13/−7
  • Diff: 4985bc5
  • Discussion: #3588490 · 7 contributors · 25 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

Every admin page load was silently invalidating the cache_bootstrap cache bin. SystemRequirementsHooks::checkRequirements() called reset() on the module and theme extension lists before reading them, wiping their cached state on each request. Because system requirements are checked on every admin page, this caused constant cache rebuilds and degraded admin performance across the board. The fix adds a way to retrieve a fresh extension list without discarding the cached copy, eliminating the unnecessary invalidation.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New optional bool $skip_cache = FALSE parameter on ExtensionList::getList() lets callers retrieve a fresh list without invalidating the cached copy.

Technical details

The root cause was inherited from Drupal 7's system_rebuild_module_data(), which both built and persisted extension data in one call. That pattern was ported forward and survived into SystemRequirementsHooks::checkRequirements(), which called $module_extension_list->reset() and $theme_extension_list->reset() before every call to getList().

ExtensionList::getList() now accepts an optional bool $skip_cache = FALSE parameter. When TRUE, it calls $this->doList() directly to bypass the persistent cache, but it still populates $this->extensions with the result so that subsequent calls within the same request use the fresh data without another rebuild. The old reset() approach cleared that property entirely, forcing every downstream caller in the same request to rebuild from scratch.

SystemRequirementsHooks::checkRequirements() drops its two ->reset() calls and switches to getList(TRUE). AppController::themesPage() similarly replaces $this->themeExtensionList->reset()->getList() with $this->themeExtensionList->getList(TRUE). The related issue #3336621 tracks the broader cost of running hook_requirements() on all admin pages.

Upgrade

If you call ExtensionList::getList() or chain ->reset()->getList() to force a fresh read, switch to the new parameter instead:

// Before
$extensions = $extensionList->reset()->getList();

// After
$extensions = $extensionList->getList(skip_cache: TRUE);

Using getList(TRUE) fetches fresh data while preserving the cached copy for subsequent callers in the same request, unlike reset() which drops the in-memory cache entirely.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 5, 2026
  • Committed: June 6, 2026 (1 month and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • godotislate

Collaboration

berdir pointed out on the MR why the original code "fails so hard right now" (the reset wipes the in-memory cache for all subsequent callers in the request, not just the persistent cache), and also cross-linked #3336621 as a complementary effort to reduce how often requirements hooks run at all. alexpott noted that the new parameter needed type hints. catch traced the pattern back to Drupal 7's system_rebuild_module_data() to explain how it survived this long.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
